overrides.override_summary: Litigation involving CA franchise must be brought in CA state or federal courts, not Georgia
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchisees can litigate locally instead of traveling to Georgia

overrides.override_summary: Post-term noncompete covenant may not be enforceable under California law
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchisees may be free to operate a competing business after termination

overrides.override_summary: General release upon renewal/transfer cannot waive rights under CA Franchise Investment Law (Corp Code §31512) or Franchise Relations Act (B&P §20010)
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchisees retain statutory claims even after signing a general release

overrides.override_summary: Georgia choice of law provision may not be enforceable under California law
overrides.why_it_matters: CA law may apply instead of Georgia law for CA franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Highest interest rate permitted by law in California is 10% annually
overrides.why_it_matters: Late payment interest capped at 10% vs. potentially higher 18%/year (1.5%/month) in standard agreement

overrides.override_summary: Liquidated damage clause in FA §11.3 may be unenforceable under Civil Code §1671
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchisees may not be bound by liquidated damages provisions

overrides.override_summary: No statement or questionnaire can waive claims under state franchise law or disclaim reliance on franchisor statements
overrides.why_it_matters: Protects CA franchisees from being bound by pre-signing acknowledgments

overrides.override_summary: Illinois law governs the franchise agreements
overrides.why_it_matters: Georgia law is overridden for IL franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Venue designation outside Illinois is void (except arbitration may be outside IL)
overrides.why_it_matters: IL franchisees cannot be forced to litigate in Georgia, though arbitration may still be in Georgia

overrides.override_summary: No waiver of compliance with Illinois Franchise Disclosure Act or other IL law
overrides.why_it_matters: IL franchisees retain all statutory protections

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration location provision deleted — arbitration will not be required outside Indiana (to extent IN law applies)
overrides.why_it_matters: IN franchisees may arbitrate in Indiana

overrides.override_summary: General release required for renewal/sale/transfer does not apply to any liability under Maryland Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law
overrides.why_it_matters: MD franchisees retain rights to sue under MD franchise law even after signing release

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may bring lawsuit in Maryland for claims under MD Franchise Law
overrides.why_it_matters: MD franchisees can sue locally for MD franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: Termination upon bankruptcy may not be enforceable under federal bankruptcy law
overrides.why_it_matters: Automatic termination for bankruptcy may be void

overrides.override_summary: Post-term noncompete unenforceable under Michigan Franchise Investment Law
overrides.why_it_matters: MI franchisees are free to compete after termination

overrides.override_summary: Release requirement upon renewal or transfer is void under MI law
overrides.why_it_matters: MI franchisees cannot be required to sign general release

overrides.override_summary: Franchisor prohibited from requiring litigation outside Minnesota
overrides.why_it_matters: MN franchisees can litigate and arbitrate in Minnesota

overrides.override_summary: Franchisor cannot require consent to liquidated damages, termination penalties, or judgment notes
overrides.why_it_matters: MN franchisees protected from liquidated damages and penalty clauses

overrides.override_summary: Release does not apply to claims under Minnesota Franchise Act
overrides.why_it_matters: MN franchisees retain statutory claims despite release

overrides.override_summary: MN law requires 90 days notice of termination (60 days to cure) and 180 days notice for nonrenewal
overrides.why_it_matters: Extended cure and notice periods beyond what FA may provide

overrides.override_summary: Washington Franchise Investment Protection Act prevails in event of conflict
overrides.why_it_matters: WA law overrides Georgia law for WA franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration/mediation must be in Washington or mutually agreed location. Litigation may be brought in Washington.
overrides.why_it_matters: WA franchisees not required to travel to Georgia

overrides.override_summary: Release/waiver void unless executed pursuant to negotiated settlement after agreement in effect with independent counsel (RCW 19.100.220(2)). Void for renewal/transfer releases.
overrides.why_it_matters: WA franchisees cannot be required to sign general release for renewal or transfer

overrides.override_summary: Noncompete void for employees earning under $100K/year (adjusted for inflation) and independent contractors under $250K/year per RCW 49.62. Any conflicting FA provision void in WA.
overrides.why_it_matters: WA significantly limits enforcement of noncompete provisions based on income thresholds

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may terminate under any grounds permitted by WA state law. Franchisor Bill of Rights (RCW 19.100.180) may supersede FA provisions on termination and renewal.
overrides.why_it_matters: WA provides additional franchisee termination rights beyond what FA allows

overrides.override_summary: Virginia-specific addendum adds risk disclosures and modifies certain FA provisions
overrides.why_it_matters: VA franchisees receive additional disclosures
